Maine Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
No Interstate Compact

How to Transfer Your Dietitian License to Maine

There is no active interstate compact covering dietitian practice in Maine. To work in Maine, apply for licensure by endorsement through Board of Licensing of Dietetic Practice.

Endorsement fee
$171
Processing time
3 to 5 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Available
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Complete Education & Supervised Practice

    Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,000 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an accredited dietitian internship.

  2. Pass the CDR Examination

    Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status satisfies this.

  3. Submit the Application via Maine OPOR

    Create an account on the Maine OPOR online portal, complete the initial application, and pay the required fees ($150 license + $21 background check).

  4. Provide Verification Documents

    Submit a copy of your active CDR card or ensure verification is sent directly. A criminal history records check is conducted automatically with the submission of your application and fee.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$171
Processing time
3 to 5 Weeks
Renewal fee
$150
Renewal cycle
Annual (by Dec 31st) + 15 CE hours

Required Documents

  • CDR Registration Card: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Criminal History Records Check: Processed internally by the Board upon receipt of the $21 fee with an application.
  • Official College Transcripts: May be required if not submitting primary source verification via the CDR.

Board Contact

Board
Board of Licensing of Dietetic Practice
Department
Maine Office of Professional and Occupational Regulation (OPOR)
Phone
(207) 624-8626
Email
dietitians.board@maine.gov

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $150. Renewal cycle: Annual (by Dec 31st) + 15 CE hours.

Maine Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

How much does a Maine dietitian license cost?

The initial cost is $171, which includes a $150 license fee and a $21 criminal history records check fee.

When does the Maine license need to be renewed?

Maine dietitian licenses expire annually on December 31st. The renewal fee is $150. A $50 late fee is added if renewed after the expiration date.

What are the continuing education requirements for Maine?

Licensees must complete 15 hours of approved continuing education annually. At least 4 of these hours must be from approved Category 1 programs.

Does Maine have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Maine. To work in Maine, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Maine?

Most endorsement applications take 3 to 5 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Maine while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
